content.Text:
All working. No TypeScript errors. Here's the summary:

**Changes made across 3 files:**

1. **`src/openmeteo.ts`** — Added 3 new exports:
   - `fetchGFSEnsembleHighs()` — hits the Open-Meteo ensemble API, returns ~31 daily max temps from GFS members
   - `computeEnsemblePWin()` — fraction of ensemble members above/below the implied temp
   - `computeEnsembleStdDev()` — standard deviation across ensemble members

2. **`src/signals.ts`** — Updated `scanCity()`:
   - Imports the 3 new functions
   - Fetches ensemble data after existing forecasts, logs member count/mean/std
   - Computes `ensemblePWin` when a signal is found
   - Adds ensemble pWin line to Telegram messages
   - Passes ensemble params to `logPaperTrade()`

3. **`src/paper.ts`** — Updated paper trading:
   - Added `ensemble_pwin`,

1	import axios from 'axios';
2	
3	/**
4	 * Open-Meteo forecast API — free, no key, uses GFS/ECMWF models
5	 * Often updates more frequently than NWS human-issued forecasts.
6	 * This is a key part of the edge: model forecast vs market implied.
7	 */
8	
9	const FORECAST_URL = 'https://api.open-meteo.com/v1/forecast';
10	const ARCHIVE_URL = 'https://archive-api.open-meteo.com/v1/archive';
11	
12	export interface CityCoords {
13	  lat: number;
14	  lon: number;
15	  timezone: string;
16	}
17	
18	// ⚠️ CRITICAL: Use exact NWS airport station coordinates, NOT city center.
19	// Kalshi settles on NWS Daily Climate Reports from these specific stations.
20	// Using city center coords causes 3-6°F forecast errors vs official readings.

22	  NYC:  { lat: 40.7772, lon: -73.8726, timezone: 'America/New_York' },   // KLGA LaGuardia
23	  CHI:  { lat: 41.7868, lon: -87.7522, timezone: 'America/Chicago' },    // KMDW Midway (Kalshi uses Midway NOT O'Hare)
